content.problemStatement:
You are given a grid of size N√óMN\times MN√óM (N(N(N and MMM are both even))), where each cell of the grid has value 000.

Let RiR_iRi‚Äč denote the bitwise XOR of all elements of the ithi^{th}ith row and CiC_iCi‚Äč denote the bitwise XOR of all elements of the ithi^{th}ith column.

Ugliness of a grid is defined as ‚ąĎi=1NRi+‚ąĎi=1MCi\displaystyle{\sum_{i=1}^{N} R_i  + \sum_{i=1}^{M} C_i}i=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčRi‚Äč+i=1‚ąĎM‚ÄčCi‚Äč.

Let F(K)F(K)F(K) denote the **minimum** ugliness possible after setting the value of **exactly** KKK cells of this grid as 111.

Find the value ‚ąĎi=1N‚čÖMF(i)\sum_{i=1}^{N \cdot M} F(i)‚ąĎi=1N‚čÖM‚ÄčF(i).


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two space-separated **even** integers N,MN, MN,M ‚ÄĒ the number of rows and the number of columns respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value ‚ąĎi=1N‚čÖMF(i)\sum_{i=1}^{N \cdot M} F(i)‚ąĎi=1N‚čÖM‚ÄčF(i).
constraints: 1‚Č§N,M‚Č§109; N1 \leq N, M \leq 10^9; \ N1‚Č§N,M‚Č§109; N and MMM are even.
